title: The Median Angle
question_latex: In $\triangle ABC$, let $M$ be the midpoint of side $BC$ (so $BM = MC$). The median $AM$ is drawn from vertex $A$ to the base. Given: $\angle BMA = 45^\circ$ and $\angle BCA = 30^\circ$. Find the measure of angle $x = \angle MBA$.
solution_latex: Since $\angle BMA$ and $\angle AMC$ are supplementary, $\angle AMC = 180^\circ - 45^\circ = 135^\circ$. In $\triangle AMC$, $\angle MAC = 180^\circ - 135^\circ - 30^\circ = 15^\circ$. Let $x = \angle MBA$. In $\triangle ABM$, $\angle BAM = 135^\circ - x$. By the Law of Sines, since $BM = MC$: $$\dfrac{\sin(135^\circ - x)}{\sin x} = \dfrac{\sin 15^\circ}{\sin 30^\circ}$$ Solving this equation gives $x = 105^\circ$. Check: in $\triangle ABM$, angles are $45^\circ + 105^\circ + 30^\circ = 180^\circ$. Confirmed.

title: Nested Radical
solution_latex: Notice the inner radical $\sqrt{2022 + x}$ has the same form as the original equation. If $x$ satisfies $x = \sqrt{2022 + x}$, it also satisfies the original. Squaring: $x^2 = 2022 + x$, which gives $x^2 - x - 2022 = 0$. By the quadratic formula (taking the positive root since $x > 0$): $$x = \frac{1 + \sqrt{1 + 4 \cdot 2022}}{2} = \frac{1 + \sqrt{8089}}{2}$$ Verification: $\sqrt{8089} \approx 89.94$, so $x \approx 45.47$. Check: $\sqrt{2022 + 45.47} \approx 45.47$. ✓

title: Exponential Equation
solution_latex: Let $u = 5^x$, so $5^{3x} = u^3$. The equation becomes $u^3 + u - 10 = 0$. Testing $u = 2$: $8 + 2 - 10 = 0$. ✓ Factor: $(u-2)(u^2+2u+5) = 0$. The quadratic has discriminant $4 - 20 = -16 < 0$, so no real roots besides $u = 2$. Since $5^x = 2$, taking $\log_5$ of both sides:

title: Integer System
question_latex: Find the positive integers $a$, $b$, and $c$, where $a<b$, given: $$\begin{aligned} ab+c &= 2022\\ abc &= 2021 \end{aligned}$$
solution_latex: Since $abc = 2021$ and $2021 = 43 \times 47$ (both prime), the positive integer factorizations are $\{1, 1, 2021\}$ and $\{1, 43, 47\}$. Testing $\{1, 43, 47\}$ with $c = 1$: $ab + c = 43 \times 47 + 1 = 2022$. ✓ The case $\{1, 1, 2021\}$ requires $a = b$, violating $a < b$.
